title: Two blank pages
why.empty: It's blank. I know. This is where most people freeze — and there's no trick for it. Write anything. One tag. One word. Code gets written by starting to write.
hint2.empty: Don't think about what's correct. Write an <h1> with a name in it. You'll fix it after.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or a quote is missing.
hint2.not-closed: Check the <link> — its values go in quotes. One to open, one to close.
hint3.not-closed: <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css> — the quote after css is missing. From there the browser reads everything as part of the value.
why.no-heading: There's no heading with text in it. You know the tag from the HTML course.
hint2.no-heading:
The tag is h1. You wrap the text:

in front,

behind.
hint3.no-heading:
If you already wrote it, check the text is INSIDE.

Bean Street

, not

Bean Street.
why.no-p: There's a heading but nothing under it. One sentence is enough.
hint2.no-p:
The tag is p. One sentence under the heading:

...

.
why.no-body: There's no body. Your page is empty or the tags don't close.
hint2.no-body: Start from the skeleton: <!DOCTYPE html>, then html, head, body.
why.not-linked: The styles aren't reaching the page. Either there's no <link>, or the path in it points at a file that doesn't exist.
hint2.not-linked: Look at the name on the second tab above the editor. Letter by letter. Then compare it with what you wrote in href.
hint3.not-linked: <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css"> — the line goes in <head>. rel says what it is, href is the path. The tag closes itself, there's no </link>.
hint4.not-linked: If you wrote the styles in a <style> inside the page — that works, but here I want you to link the file. That's exactly what I promised you in lesson 04.
why.no-font: You haven't set a font. The page is using the browser's default.
hint2.no-font: The property is font-family, and its place is body — from there it flows down to everything.
hint3.no-font: font-family: Georgia, serif; — the first is what you want, the second is the fallback if the machine doesn't have it.
why.no-color: The heading is black, meaning you never gave it a colour. Give it whatever you like — just not black.
hint2.no-color: A rule for h1 with the color property. The value is your choice.
blocks.0.text: Two tabs. Both blank. Nothing there — no skeleton, no comments, nothing to fill in.
blocks.1.text: This is where most people freeze. You know everything you need and your fingers still won't move. That's normal. You'll go through it many more times.
blocks.2.text: Make a page and dress it
blocks.3.text: No instructions. No steps. No correct answer. Only this:
blocks.4.items.0: index.html — a heading and at least one sentence
blocks.4.items.1: index.html — one line that links styles.css
blocks.4.items.3: styles.css — a colour on the heading, whichever you like
blocks.6.text: In lesson 04 I told you you'd write the <link> yourself, in your own file, and that there it would really work. Here's that moment. Get the name wrong by one letter and the page stays unstyled and nobody tells you why. Exactly like tomorrow, in real work.
blocks.7.text: I'm not checking which colour you picked. This is your page. I'm only checking you didn't leave it black.
blocks.9.text: Write anything. One tag. One name. Just don't stare at the blank screen. Code gets written by starting to write — not by sitting and working out the right way.
blocks.10.text: Other courses teach you to follow steps. This lesson teaches you to start from nothing. The second one is the hard one, and the second one is what you'll do for the rest of your life.
